source_jobs.title: Customer Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?

We’re hiring a Customer Operations Lead to run day-to-day customer operations and ensure fast, high-quality outcomes. You will own non-technical support, billing ops, triage into technical support, and a light support-to-success motion for renewals and upsells. You will also hire and manage contractors to scale support capacity.

You should be:

 - Operationally excellent: crisp processes, strong prioritization, reliable execution

 - Customer-obsessed: you drive outcomes, not just ticket closure

 - Calm under pressure: strong escalation judgment and clear communication

 - Builder + manager: you can recruit, train, and manage contractors with clear QA




WHAT WILL YOU LEAD?

 - Billing operations: refunds, disputes/chargebacks, invoices, plan changes, account changes

 - Non-technical support: handle requests when AI support fails or users need human help

 - Triage and routing: classify issues, gather context, escalate to Technical Support/Engineering with clean repro details

 - Contractor team: source, hire, onboard, schedule, and manage contractors; set QA standards, macros, and playbooks

 - SLAs and outcomes: define and run response SLAs, backlog health, and customer outcome accountability

 - Escalations + prioritization: manage hot accounts and cross-team prioritization across support and engineering

 - Light commercial motion: coordinate renewals/upsells, identify expansion signals, and route to the right owner

 - Operating cadence + tooling: weekly reviews, dashboards, workflows, and continuous process improvement




WHAT SKILLS DO WE EXPECT YOU TO HAVE?

 - Experience running customer operations or support ops in a SaaS environment

 - Strong understanding of billing and payments workflows (refunds, disputes, subscriptions)

 - Experience managing other employees or contractors

 - Excellent written communication: clear, fast, and professional customer-facing responses

 - Strong triage instincts: can separate billing vs product vs technical issues and route correctly

 - Comfort with tooling: ticketing systems, CRMs, internal dashboards, and basic analytics

 - Ability to drive process: SLAs, escalation paths, playbooks, and cross-functional coordination



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 1. We move with urgency. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates in US time zones who would be willing/able to travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - User whatever AI productivity tools you’d like

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$100k-$250k + equity (depends on seniority, equity, and location)

source_jobs.title: Technical Customer Support Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?

We’re hiring a Technical Support Engineer to help our top customers solve the hardest technical problems in apps built on Hercules. You will handle complex debugging, provide architecture guidance, and unblock high-value users fast.



You should be:

 - Strong technically: you can reason about full-stack systems and debug unfamiliar code quickly.

 - Customer-oriented: clear communicator who can translate complexity into next steps.

 - Fast and thorough: you triage efficiently, reproduce issues, and drive to resolution.

 - Product-minded: you turn recurring issues into fixes, docs, and automation.




WHAT WILL YOU LEAD?

 - Own escalations: take the most technical support tickets and unblock users quickly

 - Deep debugging: reproduce issues, inspect logs, isolate root causes, and propose fixes/workarounds

 - Architecture guidance: advise on app structure, data modeling, auth, payments, performance, and deployment patterns

 - Incident handling: diagnose platform issues vs user issues, coordinate internally, communicate status clearly

 - Knowledge base: write high-signal docs, runbooks, and troubleshooting guides

 - Automation: build tooling to detect common failures, generate better diagnostics, and reduce repeat tickets

 - Feedback loop: surface product gaps, propose improvements, and help implement fixes where needed




WHAT SKILLS DO WE EXPECT YOU TO HAVE?

 - Strong TypeScript and modern web fundamentals (HTTP, APIs, auth, databases)

 - Proven ability to debug production issues (logs, traces, repros, minimal test cases)

 - Strong systems thinking: architecture (scalability, reliability, performance, security basics)

 - Excellent written communication: concise explanations, clear action plans, good incident updates

 - Experience working in a customer facing role (sales, pre-sales, customer support, etc)

 - Ideally experience supporting developers or working in a high-velocity product environment



Will you always be a technical support engineer?

If you’d like to, yes. However, after 6-12 months we are open to working with you to transition you to a full time software engineering, pre-sales, or post-sales engineering role



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 1. We move with urgency. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates in US time zones who would be willing/able to travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - Use whatever AI productivity tools you’d like

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$80k-$175k USD + equity (depends on seniority, equity, and location)

source_jobs.title: Performance Marketing Lead (Paid Growth)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Own paid growth end-to-end across Google (Search/YouTube), Meta (Instagram / Facebook), TikTok, Reddit, X, LinkedIn, and more



Who you are

 - Performance-first + accountable: CAC + payback.

 - Extremely data-driven: funnels → cohorts → payback

 - Experimentation-obsessed: rapid, structured testing across creative/audience/offer/LPs.

 - Creative: strong angles

 - AI-leveraged: scale ad creation + iteration with AI tools like HeyGen,

 - Experienced in SaaS/software
   
   

What you’ll do

 - Own channel strategy + budgets across the platforms above.

 - Build campaign architecture (targeting, bidding, pacing, landing pages).

 - Run a creative machine (UGC-style concepts, hooks, copy, statics, short-form video).

 - Own measurement + tracking from click → paid user; improve attribution quality.

 - Automate ops/reporting/analysis to move faster with less manual work.



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 1. We move with urgency. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.



Is this a full-time role or contractor?

We are open to either.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

 - For full-time: we would prefer in-office in San Francisco but are open to remote in US timezones.

 - For contractors: remote in US time-zones



What are Hercules’ benefits for full-time employees?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - User whatever AI tools you’d like (you have an unlimited AI budget)

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$100k-$250k (+ equity for full-time)

source_jobs.title: Content & Social Lead
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

We’re hiring a Content and Social Lead to own organic growth end-to-end: thought leadership, social channels, newsletter, and video. Your job is to build a repeatable content engine that consistently drives awareness, traffic, and signups.



What will you lead?

 - All Hercules' organic content
   
   - Channels: X/Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok, Newsletter, Blog
   
   - Content type: thought leadership and product/feature launch content
   
   - Scope: strategy, calendar, distribution, and iteration

 - Contractors pipeline: hire/manage writers, editors, designers, creators; create briefs; ensure high quality output, ensure we meet deadlines

 - Video production: direct vendors for launch videos + testimonial/customer videos (briefs, scripts, review, delivery)

 - Asset repurposing: turn every asset into many (threads, clips, posts, email, blog) with quality control



What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Proven track record growing organic social for a product (ideally developer tools, SaaS, or prosumer)

 - Excellent writing and editorial judgment (hooks, clarity, voice, pacing)

 - Strong creative direction for short-form video (scripting, structure, creator guidance)

 - Ability to build and manage a content production system (briefs, QA, workflows, vendor management)

 - Comfort with analytics (what moved, why it moved, and what to do next)

 - Strong AI workflows for ideation, drafting, editing, repurposing, and performance analysis



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 1. We move with urgency. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates in US time zones who would be willing/able to travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - User whatever AI tools you’d like (you have an unlimited AI budget)

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$100k-$250k + equity (depends on seniority, equity, and location)

source_jobs.title: Technical Community Lead
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?
We’re hiring a technical Community Lead to own our public forum and keep users unblocked. You are hands-on: you can debug, give architecture guidance, and jump on calls when needed. You also build scalable community systems (automation, ambassadors, events) and run the feedback loop into product/support.



What will you lead?

 - Forum ownership + support routing: ensure every thread gets answered or redirected correctly (docs, support ticket, technical escalation)

 - Hands-on technical help: help users build apps, debug common issues, provide fixes/workarounds, and hop on calls to unblock

 - Automation + workflows: build systems that make support fast and scalable (macros, templates, AI workflows, routing)

 - Community programs: ambassadors, rewards/recognition, office hours, AMAs, hackathons

 - Feedback loop: triage/categorize/summarize themes and feed them into roadmap decisions; build lightweight fixes for recurring issues when needed
   
   

What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Strong TypeScript (you can actually debug and advise)

 - Strong AI skills (prompting, agent workflows, debugging AI behavior)

 - Excellent written and verbal communication

 - High-energy, creative, community-first

 - Nice-to-have: experience running/scaling an online community



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates in US time zones who would be willing/able to travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - User whatever AI tools you’d like (you have an unlimited AI budget)

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$80k-$180k + equity (depends on seniority, equity, and location)

source_jobs.title: Creator Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

We’re hiring a Creator Partnerships Lead to build and scale Hercules’ creator distribution. You will own partnerships end-to-end, from sourcing to measurement, and turn creator content into reusable growth assets.

You should be:

 - Deal-oriented: strong at outreach, negotiation, and closing.

 - Taste-driven: you know which creators will perform and why.

 - Process-strong: you can run a high-volume pipeline without chaos.

 - Performance-aware: you treat creator spend like a growth channel with clear KPIs.



What will you lead?

 - Sponsored creator and influencer program across major and micro channels

 - Sourcing + outreach: identify creators, run inbound/outbound, maintain pipeline

 - Negotiation + contracts: rates, terms, deliverables, timelines, and compliance

 - Briefs + approvals: creative direction, messaging, brand safety, review workflow

 - Deliverable ops: timelines, revisions, posting verification, link/UTM setup

 - Tracking + reporting: performance by creator, format, platform, and cohort quality



What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Proven experience running creator/influencer partnerships with measurable outcomes

 - Strong networking and sourcing ability (finding emerging creators before they are expensive)

 - Excellent negotiation and contract instincts (rates, rights, exclusivity, timelines)

 - Strong creative briefing skills (clear direction without constraining creators)

 - Familiarity with whitelisting/paid usage rights and how creator content performs in ads

 - Strong ops + analytics: pipelines, tracking, UTMs, reporting, and learning loops

 - Good judgment on branding (especially with meme and micro media pages)



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done. We are politely relentless.

source_jobs.title: Channel Partnerships & Affiliates Lead
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

We’re hiring a Channel Partnerships and Affiliates Lead to build Hercules’ partner distribution engine. You will own partner acquisition, program design, enablement, and performance.

You should be:

 - Commercial: strong at pitching, negotiating, and closing.

 - Systems-minded: you build programs that scale with clean tracking and tight guardrails.

 - Partner-obsessed: you make partners successful with clear enablement and co-marketing.

 - Metrics-driven: you manage partners to sourced signups, revenue, CAC/payback, and quality.



What will you lead?

 - Channel distribution deals: identify targets, build the pitch, negotiate terms, close partnerships

 - Affiliate program: define structure (tiers, incentives, attribution window), recruit affiliates, and scale

 - Tracking + payouts: attribution, fraud prevention, payout rules, and reporting

 - Partner enablement: onboarding, messaging, assets, demo flows, and ongoing support

 - Co-marketing: launches, webinars, newsletters, marketplace listings, and joint announcements

 - Partner pipeline + relationship management: CRM pipeline, cadence, QBRs (quarterly business reviews), renewals/expansions



What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Experience closing and scaling channel partnerships and/or affiliate programs

 - Strong deal execution: outreach, negotiation, contracting, and stakeholder management

 - Strong program design: incentives, guardrails, attribution windows, payout policies

 - Familiarity with affiliate tracking and payout ops (links, UTMs, coupons, postbacks, anti-fraud)

 - Ability to drive partner enablement and co-marketing without heavy internal overhead

 - Strong analytical skills: partner-level performance, cohort quality, and unit economics tracking



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

 1. We move with urgency. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

source_jobs.title: Channel Partnerships & Affiliates Lead
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

We’re hiring a Channel Partnerships and Affiliates Lead to build Hercules’ partner distribution engine. You will own partner acquisition, program design, enablement, and performance.

You should be:

 - Commercial: strong at pitching, negotiating, and closing.

 - Systems-minded: you build programs that scale with clean tracking and tight guardrails.

 - Partner-obsessed: you make partners successful with clear enablement and co-marketing.

 - Metrics-driven: you manage partners to sourced signups, revenue, CAC/payback, and quality.



What will you lead?

 - Channel distribution deals: identify targets, build the pitch, negotiate terms, close partnerships

 - Affiliate program: define structure (tiers, incentives, attribution window), recruit affiliates, and scale

 - Tracking + payouts: attribution, fraud prevention, payout rules, and reporting

 - Partner enablement: onboarding, messaging, assets, demo flows, and ongoing support

 - Co-marketing: launches, webinars, newsletters, marketplace listings, and joint announcements

 - Partner pipeline + relationship management: CRM pipeline, cadence, QBRs (quarterly business reviews), renewals/expansions



What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Experience closing and scaling channel partnerships and/or affiliate programs

 - Strong deal execution: outreach, negotiation, contracting, and stakeholder management

 - Strong program design: incentives, guardrails, attribution windows, payout policies

 - Familiarity with affiliate tracking and payout ops (links, UTMs, coupons, postbacks, anti-fraud)

 - Ability to drive partner enablement and co-marketing without heavy internal overhead

 - Strong analytical skills: partner-level performance, cohort quality, and unit economics tracking



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

source_jobs.title: AI-native Growth Hacker
source_jobs.description:
Hercules is hiring AI-native growth hackers. Your job is to help us grow from 100k users and $XM of revenue to 100M users and $100M+ revenue as quickly as possible. You will have an unlimited AI budget.



We are looking for true AI power users. Not people who use ChatGPT to be 10 to 20% more productive. We want people who can use AI plus code to 100x themselves and do things that were not possible two years ago.



If you are a growth hacker at the cutting edge of AI, with a voracious appetite for experimentation, and you want the budget to scale your best playbook, this role is for you.



What you will do

 - Pick one specific growth wedge and own it end-to-end (programmatic SEO, paid ads, lifecycle, etc.).

 - Build an AI-driven system to execute it at scale.

 - Run tight loops: ship, measure, learn, repeat.

 - Turn what works into durable infrastructure we keep scaling.



What skills you need need

 - Exceptional AI skills (asynchronous agents, automations, evals and QA).

 - Strong communication.

 - Ability to code (TypeScript-first).

 - Track record running successful, large-scale growth strategies



How we operate

 - Urgency: ship fast, iterate, correct quickly.

 - Excellence: have taste, get the details right, make outputs simple and self-serve.

 - Hunger: work hard, do what it takes, be politely relentless.



Your application

Pick ONE specific growth play that will have the highest impact and describe it below (examples: programmatic SEO, TikTok ads, partnerships, lifecycle email).

In your answer, include:

 1. The play: What exactly is your play? How does it work? What are the outputs?

 2. How you will use AI to 100x impact: which AI tools/workflows will you use? How will you ensure high quality?

 3. Measurement: What metrics will you track? How will you iterate / experiment?

 4. CAC Payback: How you will ensure this play is profitable?

If we like you and your idea we will quite literally hire you on the spot to go implement this play.



Compensation

$100k to $250k plus equity (based on seniority and location).

source_jobs.title: Developer Relations (DevRel)
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?
We’re hiring a DevRel to own our public community and create unique, engaging content.



Who are you?

 - You are hands-on: you can debug, give architecture guidance, and jump on calls when needed

 - You are creative: you can come up with unique, engaging content

 - You are a great communicator and presenter: you can give demos, give talks, create videos.

 - You are product minded: you can take repeated feedback, turn it into product ideas, and build the solution.



What will you lead?

 - Public forum: You help manage, support, and grow our public community

 - Content creation: You create high quality, short-and-long form content (blog/video/whatever you are best at)

 - Hands-on technical help: help users build apps, debug common issues, provide fixes/workarounds, and hop on calls to unbloc

 - Community programs: ambassadors, rewards/recognition, office hours, AMAs, hackathons

 - Feedback loop: triage/categorize/summarize themes and feed them into roadmap decisions; build lightweight fixes for recurring issues when needed
   
   

What skills do we expect you to have?

 - Strong TypeScript (you can actually debug and advise)

 - Strong AI skills (prompting, agent workflows, debugging AI behavior)

 - Excellent written and verbal communication

 - High-energy, creative, community-first

 - Experience running/scaling an online community



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of them are specifically choices that we as a company unique prioritize and so can be learned on the job. However, we do specifically filter for these principles our hiring process:

Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates in US time zones who would be willing/able to travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - User whatever AI tools you’d like (you have an unlimited AI budget)

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here

$80k-$180k + equity (depends on seniority, equity, and location)

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

Hercules https://hercules.app is looking for a senior or staff level product software engineer to help us build the future of AI software development



What will you do?

You will build and own user-facing features across Hercules including our

 - AI Agent, the AI harness that enables anyone to build powerful full-stack applications

 - App Builder, the interface that makes it ridiculously easy to build, preview, and deploy apps

 - SDK, the headless SaaS that users can integrate into their apps like Hercules Commerce, Hercules Auth, Hercules CMS, Hercules Files & Media,

 - Mobile app, our PWA-based mobile building experience

 - Platform, our user friendly experience with enterprise-grade security and reliability



What skills do we expect you to have?

We expect candidates to be incredibly strong across all of the following areas:

 - Software fundamentals: system design, architecture, data modeling, and API design

 - Code and codebase quality: write clean, maintainable code. Work effectively in monorepos

 - Product sense: track record of independently shipping lots of features to customers

 - Design sense: beautiful sense of UX/UI using cutting-edge frontend innovations

 - SaaS: general experience with billing (subscriptions, entitlements), product analytics (A/B testing, feature flags), and other popular SaaS features (like i18n, notifications) for large user bases

 - Our core stack: TypeScript, React, containers, Postgres, and Redis,

 - AI: able to truly 10x your workflow by being on frontier of AI coding



What’s Hercules’ stack?

 - Technologies: Typescript, React, AI SDK, Postgres, Redis, Clickhouse, Tailwind, IaC (CDK, Terraform), Docker, Otel, Hono, Drizzle ORM, Temporal

 - Clouds: Cloudflare, AWS

 - AI Models: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of the principles can be learned on the job, however, we do specifically filter for the following principles in our hiring process:

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done.

What are Hercules’ benefits?

source_jobs.title: SEO Content Writer
source_jobs.description:
What are we looking for?

Hercules https://hercules.app is looking for a senior or staff level SEO Content Writer to help us educate the world on what Hercules is, how it works, and why it matters.



What will you do?

You will own Hercules’ SEO content engine end to end.

 1. Identify the highest-value keywords and search opportunities to target
    
    1. e.g. "best AI app builder" or "hercules vs lovable" or "build a CRM"

 2. Publish long-form content that is simple, accurate, clear, and useful

 3. Monitor and optimize existing content until it ranks highly and drives traffic/signups/revenue

 4. Automate your workflow with AI



What skills do we expect you to have?

We expect candidates to be incredibly strong across all of the following areas:

 - Writing quality: exceptional long-form writing ability. Our style is terse, simple, clear, convincing, authentic, and useful. We do not accept AI slop. Strong communication is a #1 requirement

 - SEO: strong understanding of search intent, content structure, on-page SEO, internal linking, content refreshes, and what it takes for content to rank

 - Commercial sense: write content that ranks AND converts the audience to paid customers

 - Technical fluency: familiarity with technical concepts like APIs, authentication, hosting, role based access control etc

 - AI leverage: able to truly 10x your workflow by using the frontier of AI tools well. We expect you to be able to product ~50+ high quality articles a month, if not more



What are Hercules’ operating principles?

Hercules’ operating principles guide how we make decisions every day. We take them very seriously. Most of the principles can be learned on the job, however, we do specifically filter for the following principles in our hiring process:

 1. We ship. We ship fast, iterate, and correct mistakes quickly. Our bias for action speeds up our learning and delights our customers.

 2. We strive for excellence. Doing things well is in our DNA. We have good taste. We get the details right. Our outputs are exceptionally simple and self-serve. We’re proud of our work.

 3. We are hungry. We are willing to work hard and break through walls to achieve the outcomes we want. We are willing to operate at any level to get things done.



Is Hercules in-office or remote?

Hercules founding team works in-office in San Francisco (Kearny and Bush). We have a very strong preference for in office. We are open to remote for exceptional candidates who is willing to 1) work in US time zones and 2) travel to the office regularly.



What are Hercules’ benefits?

 - Unlimited AI token budget

 - Take what you need vacation policy

 - Healthcare, vision, and dental

 - Daily lunches, dinners and snacks in the office

 - We guarantee you will do the best work of your career here



What’s the compensation?

$100k-$250k + equity (variable based on seniority, equity, and location)

notes: High volume of technical roles, but also significant number of non-technical roles. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
